notes: COLLEGE ADMISSIONS REQUIREMENTS FOR TRANSFER STUDENTS THIS MAJOR IS OFFERED BY THE COLLEGE OF LETTERS AND SCIENCE (L&S) By the end of the spring term preceding fall enrollment at Berkeley, you must complete: 1) The L&S Essential Skills Requirements: Reading & Composition, Quantitative Reasoning, and Foreign Language; OR 2) IGETC IMPORTANT information for students applying for admission to L&S for the fall 2025 application cycle and beyond Completion of Essential Skills requirements (R&C, QR, and FL) or IGETC will no longer be a requirement for admission; however, completion of the Essential Skills requirements is strongly recommended prior to enrollment at UC Berkeley. For more information on minimum UC eligibility requirements for transfer applicants for fall 2025 and beyond, please visit the UC Berkeley transfer requirements page . Major Requirements: See details on preparation for this major below. Primary selection criteria for admission, in general: - completion of L&S Requirements (or IGETC), plus - strength of academic preparation, and - grade point average. For more information on admission to UC Berkeley: http://admissions.berkeley.edu For more information on majors at UC Berkeley: Berkeley Academic Guide: http://guide.berkeley.edu

● A score of 4 or 5 on the Advanced Placement Statistics Exam can satisfy the Statistics/Logic requirement for the Sociology major. Students must be prepared to provide their AP score report in order to use exam credit for the Statistics/Logic major requirement.

Sociology Undergraduate Program website: https://sociology.berkeley.edu/undergraduate-program Sociology Major Map https://vcue.berkeley.edu/sites/default/files/sociology.pdf Major Requirements https://sociology.berkeley.edu/major-requirements Previous Course Syllabi https://sociology.berkeley.edu/resources/syllabi-main-page For more information: Department of Sociology Cristina Rojas, Undergraduate Advisor Seng Saelee, Undergraduate Advisor (510) 642-4766 socadv@berkeley.edu http://sociology.berkeley.edu

Fall 2023 Admission for this Major: Transfer students who select this major on their College of Letters and Science (L&S) application and are offered admission to UCB L&S will be directly admitted into this major. If you are interested in this major, it is very important to select this major on your application, and complete as many lower-division major requirements as possible prior to transfer. See details on preparation for this major below. REQUIRED COURSES: Complete the approved lower division courses or their equivalents as listed in the “Lower Division Major Preparation Courses” section of this document: · Sociology 1 (Introduction to Sociology) and · One course in either statistics or logic. NOTE: The Sociology major has a total of 3 lower division prerequisites. Although some community colleges offer the equivalent of UCB’s Sociology 5 (Evaluation of Evidence), most do not. If an equivalent for UCB’s Sociology 5 is not available at your California Community College, it is recommended that transfer students take UCB’s Sociology 5 course in their first semester at UC Berkeley. See “Strongly Recommended” section below for more information. STRONGLY RECOMMENDED Although some California community colleges offer the equivalent of UCB’s Sociology 5 (Evaluation of Evidence), most do not. It is recommended that transfer students take UCB’s Sociology 5 course in their first semester at UC Berkeley unless it is available at their community college. For a list of California Community Colleges that offer an equivalent course, please visit the Sociology Major Requirements website and review the information available about the UCB Sociology 5, Evaluation of Evidence requirement. https://sociology.berkeley.edu/major-requirements Major Preparation · Entering transfer students should plan to complete any remaining prerequisites during their first semester at UC Berkeley. · Students must maintain a cumulative and major grade point average of a 2.0 or higher. · Sociology 1 or its equivalent must be completed with a letter grade of at least a D- or higher. · Sociology 5 or its equivalent must be completed with a letter grade of at least a C- or higher. · Statistics or logic may be completed with a letter grade of D- or higher, or for a “Pass” grade. · Students can remain declared in the major while the last of your prerequisites are in progress at UC Berkeley. · If you have completed course work that has not been approved for articulation with the Berkeley requirements listed below, retain syllabi and readings lists for evaluation by the department. Contact a major advisor for more information. NOTE: The Department of Sociology is not involved in the Admissions process. Students should review UC Berkeley Admissions information in order to determine how to become competitively eligible. For more information on admissions requirements to UC Berkeley, visit the Office of Undergraduate Admissions website: http://admissions.berkeley.edu
